name: Service Registry Permissions Weakness Check
notes:
This rule detects adversaries checking for flaws in permissions inside the registry to redirect from the originally specified executable to one that they control, in order to launch their own code at Service start.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: frack113
SIGMA Title: Service Registry Permissions Weakness Check
SIGMA Description: dversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the Registry entries used by services.
    Adversaries may use flaws in the permissions for registry to redirect from the originally specified executable to one that they control, in order to launch their own code at Service start.
    Windows stores local service configuration information in the Registry under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success, Exploit.Command Execution
when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%get-acl%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%REGISTRY::HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Service Registry Permissions Weakness Check on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success, Exploit.Command Execution
AND when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%get-acl%' AND LOWER("Command") LIKE '%REGISTRY::HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Service Registry Permissions Weakness Check

Event description: Detects adversaries checking for flaws in permissions inside the registry to redirect from the originally specified executable to one that they control, in order to launch their own code at Service start.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
